key_themes: Solid revenue growth and 2026 Adjusted EBITDA target of at least $400M
Margin pressure from elevated input/fruit costs and supply chain challenges
Strategic divestiture of Fresh Vegetables business and new product innovation
Stable dividend yield (~2.2%) with long-term value/undervaluation narratives
Cautious analyst consensus (Hold ratings) with average PT implying modest upside
summary: Dole plc posted strong FY2025 results with $9.2B revenue (+8.2% YoY), Adjusted EBITDA of $395M (ahead of guidance), and net income of $82M. The company is targeting at least $400M Adjusted EBITDA in 2026 after divesting its Fresh Vegetables unit for $140M and launching new products, though higher costs continue to pressure margins. Shares trade around $15.17 with a ~2.2% dividend yield; analysts maintain a Hold consensus with average targets near $17.55 (modest upside), while social/investor chatter remains quiet and focused on long-term stability rather than excitement.[[1]](https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/DOLE/)[[2]](https://www.doleplc.com/news/company-news/company-news-details/2026/Dole-plc-Reports-Fourth-Quarter-and-Full-Year-2025-Financial-Results/default.aspx)[[3]](https://seekingalpha.com/news/4557010-dole-targets-400m-adjusted-ebitda-in-2026-following-140m-fresh-vegetables-divestiture-and-new)
sources: 10+ news articles and company reports (FY2025 results, Q4 earnings, 2026 guidance, analyst notes), 8-10 relevant X posts (primarily low-engagement discussions on earnings, downgrade, and dividends)
